ddls:
CREATE TABLE "security_events" (
  "event_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each security event */,
  "event_code" INTEGER /* Event code (e.g., 4624 for successful logon) */,
  "event_type" INTEGER /* Type of event (e.g., 0 for informational) */,
  "op_code" TEXT /* Operational code (e.g., Info, Error) */,
  "record_number" INTEGER /* Record number in the event log */,
  "event_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of the event */,
  "computer_name" TEXT /* Anonymized name of the computer where the event occurred */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the event (e.g., WinEventLog:Security) */,
  "sourcetype" TEXT /* Type of event source (e.g., WinEventLog:Security) */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user involved in the event */,
  "event_outcome" TEXT /* Outcome of the event (e.g., success, failure) */,
  "event_details" TEXT /* Additional details about the event in a structured format */,
  "event_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the event, if applicable */,
  "event_source_ip" TEXT /* IP address of the event source */,
  "event_target_ip" TEXT /* IP address of the event target */,
  "event_target_name" TEXT /* Name of the event target */,
  "event_context" TEXT /* Contextual information about the event */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("event_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_security_events_sourcetype FOREIGN KEY ("sourcetype") REFERENCES source_types ("sourcetype_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_security_events_op_code FOREIGN KEY ("op_code") REFERENCES op_codes ("op_code"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_security_events_computer_name FOREIGN KEY ("computer_name") REFERENCES computers ("computer_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_security_events_source FOREIGN KEY ("source") REFERENCES event_sources ("source_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_security_events_event_type FOREIGN KEY ("event_type") REFERENCES event_types ("event_type"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_security_events_event_code FOREIGN KEY ("event_code") REFERENCES event_codes ("event_code"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_security_events_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "event_codes" (
  "event_code" INTEGER /* Event code (e.g., 4624 for successful logon) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the event code */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the event (e.g., Info, Warning, Error) */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the event code (e.g., authentication, network) */,
  "recommended_action" TEXT /* Recommended action for handling the event */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("event_code")
)

CREATE TABLE "event_types" (
  "event_type" INTEGER /* Type of event (e.g., 0 for informational) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the event type */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the event type (e.g., informational, critical) */,
  "impact_level" TEXT /* Potential impact level of the event (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("event_type")
)

CREATE TABLE "op_codes" (
  "op_code" TEXT /* Operational code (e.g., Info, Error) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the operational code */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the operational code (e.g., informational, error) */,
  "recommended_action" TEXT /* Recommended action for handling the operational code */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("op_code")
)

CREATE TABLE "computers" (
  "computer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each computer */,
  "computer_name" TEXT /* Name of the computer */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the computer */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the computer in the network */,
  "contact_info" TEXT /* Contact information for the computer's administrator */,
  "os_version" TEXT /* Operating system version of the computer */,
  "last_update" TEXT /* Last update date of the computer */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the computer (e.g., active, decommissioned) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("computer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, user, auditor) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "position" TEXT /* Position of the user within the organization */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login date and time of the user */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the account */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the user account was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "event_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each event source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the event source (e.g., WinEventLog:Security) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the event source */,
  "source_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the source type */,
  "source_version" TEXT /* Version of the event source */,
  "last_update" TEXT /* Last update date of the event source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_event_sources_source_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_type_id") REFERENCES source_types ("sourcetype_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "source_types" (
  "sourcetype_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each source type */,
  "sourcetype_name" TEXT /* Name of the source type (e.g., WinEventLog:Security) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the source type */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the source type (e.g., Windows, Linux) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sourcetype_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "event_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "event_id" INTEGER /* ID of the event the log belongs to */,
  "log_data" TEXT /* Detailed log data for the event */,
  "log_size" INTEGER /* Size of the log data in bytes */,
  "upload_date" TEXT /* Date the log was uploaded */,
  "log_format" TEXT /* Format of the log data (e.g., JSON, XML) */,
  "log_source" TEXT /* Source of the log data (e.g., file, API) */,
  "log_version" TEXT /* Version of the log data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_event_logs_event_id FOREIGN KEY ("event_id") REFERENCES security_events ("event_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "event_correlations" (
  "correlation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each event correlation */,
  "event_id_1" INTEGER /* ID of the first event in the correlation */,
  "event_id_2" INTEGER /* ID of the second event in the correlation */,
  "correlation_type" TEXT /* Type of correlation (e.g., related, causative) */,
  "correlation_description" TEXT /* Description of the correlation */,
  "correlation_score" INTEGER /* Score indicating the strength of the correlation */,
  "correlation_confidence" TEXT /* Confidence level of the correlation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("correlation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_event_correlations_event_id_1 FOREIGN KEY ("event_id_1") REFERENCES security_events ("event_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_event_correlations_event_id_2 FOREIGN KEY ("event_id_2") REFERENCES security_events ("event_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "event_id" INTEGER /* ID of the event being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the event */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the event was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "access_ip" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "access_device" TEXT /* Device used for access (e.g., desktop, mobile) */,
  "access_location" TEXT /* Location from which the access was made */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_event_id FOREIGN KEY ("event_id") REFERENCES security_events ("event_id")
)
